summary: boardflow preinstall hook drops and executes remote binary from pooron.org
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` hook executes an obfuscated `install.js` that downloads `https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe` to a temporary file (`tester_<randomhex>.exe`), chmods it to 755, and spawns it detached with `stdio:'ignore'` and `windowsHide:true` across Windows, macOS, and Linux. There is no hash or signature verification of the fetched binary, and the destination domain is unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a 'lightweight kanban board utility'). The dropper logic is hidden behind obfuscator.io-style string-array dispatching with hex-named variables (`_0x165e34`, `_0x12fa`, `_0x2dcb`) so the URL, filename, and spawn parameters are not readable in source. The package's advertised kanban CLI in `bin/boardflow.js` and `src/commands` is a thin facade; the substantive auto-executed code is the dropper. Installing this package fully compromises the installer's machine with attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
